channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Fri, 03 Jul 2026 08:55:01 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Tibetan protester sets himself on fire outside UN headquarters in New York City
item.description: A man, identified as Lobga Rangzen, set himself ablaze outside the UN headquarters in Manhattan, carrying a Tibetan flag in a suspected protest. Witnesses reported seeing him engulfed in flames before being quickly attended to by emergency services. He was pronounced dead at a hospital. Leaflets found at the scene bore slogans like "China Out of Tibet," reflecting a long-standing independence movement.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 68 women MPs in Japan had 2 toilets at parliament. After petition they have 4
item.description: Japan's parliament is set to add two more women's toilet cubicles following a petition by 58 female MPs highlighting the shortage. This move addresses the under-representation of women in politics, where they hold only 68 out of 465 lower house seats. The expansion aims to alleviate long queues faced by female lawmakers and staff, reflecting a broader push for gender equality in public life.

item.title: "Charlie never got his wish of finding romantic love": Candace Owens reignites Erika Kirk controversy with emotional Charlie Kirk claims
item.description: Candace Owens is facing renewed criticism after a viral interview clip resurfaced in which she reflected on Charlie Kirk's personal life and claimed he never found the love he had long wanted. Her remarks also referenced his widow, Erika Kirk, prompting backlash on social media. Critics accused Owens of making unverified personal claims during a sensitive time, while supporters defended her comments as an expression of grief. Neither Erika Kirk nor Charlie Kirk's family has publicly responded.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei to skip father Ali Khamenei's funeral over security fears: Report
item.description: Iran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, will skip his father's funeral due to Israeli threats, according to a representative. This follows a stark warning from Iran's Foreign Minister to the US, citing Israeli remarks about Mojtaba Khamenei being "marked for death." Diplomatic talks in Doha reported positive progress on a Middle East MoU, with further discussions planned post-funeral. Ali Khamenei was killed in an airstrike on February 28.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce already secretly married ahead of Madison Square Garden bash: Report
item.description: Pop sensation Taylor Swift and NFL star Travis Kelce have reportedly exchanged vows in a private ceremony, according to a New York Post report. Sources suggest the couple, both 36, are now legally married, with a grand celebration for over a thousand guests planned at Madison Square Garden later this week. This intimate union precedes a larger event, hinting at a significant milestone for the high-profile pair.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Is Kai Cenat set to stream Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding? Truth behind the Madison Square Garden rumor
item.description: A viral social media claim that Kai Cenat will live-stream Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding has been proven false. The rumor originated from a parody X account that openly says it posts fake stories. While speculation continues about a possible Madison Square Garden wedding, neither Swift nor Kelce has officially confirmed the venue, date or event details.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Religious proverb of the day from Thailand: "Paste gold leaf onto the back of a Buddha statue" inspires us to do good when no one is watching
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Latin proverb of the day: 'Let the experiment be made on a low-value body'
item.description: A historical Latin phrase, "fiat experimentum in corpore vili," meaning "let the experiment be made on a low-value body," reveals a troubling past where scientific discovery prioritized knowledge over human dignity. Though explicitly rejected today, its legacy echoes in modern debates about risk distribution in research and medicine, forcing us to confront how progress is achieved and who bears its cost.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Beijing pushes for a China-Pakistan-style economic corridor with Bangladesh and Myanmar
item.description: China is pushing for an economic corridor linking Bangladesh and Myanmar, mirroring CPEC's strategic importance. This initiative, discussed during Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman's China visit, aims to boost connectivity, with China developing the Mongla port. Beijing has also expressed openness to other nations joining this "transnational initiative," which has drawn India's attention due to its proximity to the eastern border.
item.dc:creator: Ahsan Tasnim

item.title: Being American means nothing: MAGA influencer slams children of H-1B standing in front of a Hindu temple ahead of July 4
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Arabic proverb of the day: 'The monkey is a gazelle in its mother's eyes'
item.description: An enchanting Arabic proverb states, 'The monkey is a gazelle in its mother's eyes,' capturing the idea that love significantly impacts how we perceive others. This adage, rooted in oral heritage, emphasizes that parents often view their children with a loving bias, making it easy to overlook their shortcomings. The vivid metaphor suggests that emotional connections can cloud our judgments, a notion reflected in contemporary psychological studies.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indian-origin man convicted of stabbing wife in Canada; claimed he accidentally ‘poked’ her
item.description: Jagpreet Singh, an Indian national, has been sentenced for second-degree murder after fatally stabbing his wife, Balwinder Kaur, only six days post-arrival in Canada. The court rejected Singh's assertion that the stabbing was accidental during a dispute, labeling his testimony as untrustworthy. Previously married for twenty years, Kaur had sponsored Singh's visa, and the tragic event took place on March 15, 2024, after she expressed concerns about his arrival.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Napoleon Bonaparte: "The world suffers a lot, not because of the violence of bad people, but because of…"
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Angela Merkel: "A good compromise is one where everybody makes a contribution" - understand why most compromises fail
